note.en: Composite route — major waypoints only. The Rihla covered ~75,000 miles and dozens of intermediate cities; this nine-segment line is a reading of the journey, not a literal track.

body.en: From Tangier (1325) → Cairo, Damascus → Mecca for Hajj → East Africa (Kilwa) → India (Delhi, ~8 years as qadi) → China (Zaytun/Quanzhou as Sultan Tughluq's envoy) → return westward → Mali (Timbuktu, 1352–53) → Tangier (1354). Roughly 30 years and three pilgrimages to Mecca along the way.
